page: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities in bzip3 tests. Passmark memory and CPU encryption tests also rank in the top 25 percent, reflecting efficient cryptographic processing. However, multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput fall within the average range. Large-scale workloads, such as large language model inference with a 70B parameter model, show weak performance, though text generation with smaller 135M models remains strong.

The B32as_v2 offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization due to its burstable CPU allocation model. This resource-density configuration is highly efficient for non-continuous workloads but presents a tradeoff during sustained high-compute demands where CPU credits may be depleted. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads, development and testing environments, low-to-medium traffic web servers, and microservices that experience periodic spikes in demand.
description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or onboard GPUs. Operating under a burstable CPU allocation model, this instance is designed for cost efficiency during variable workloads. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance and cryptographic execution, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The lack of local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage. This server is best suited for development environments, build servers, and applications with intermittent CPU demands, such as low-to-medium traffic web servers and database testing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 128.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for cost-effective, variable-demand workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable VM with 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, and 128.0 GB of RAM, ideal for variable general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A burstable, AMD-powered virtual machine offering balanced memory and compute resources for workloads with variable CPU utilization.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost-effective handling of variable workloads.
Supports Premium Storage for external disk attachments; no local storage is included.
Demonstrates strong single-core CPU and cryptographic performance in benchmarks.
Best suited for development environments, build pipelines, and low-to-medium traffic web servers.
